from dataclasses import dataclass
from collections import defaultdict

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a single fluent sentence.
    Unknown predicates fall back to a generic “subject predicate object” pattern.
    """
    # Mapping of predicates to readable sentence fragments
    templates = {
        "architecturalStyle": "{s} features {o} architectural style",
        "buildingStartDate": "{s} started construction in {o}",
        "completionDate": "{s} was completed in {o}",
        "floorCount": "{s} has {o} floors",
        "location": "{s} is located at {o}",
        "cost": "{s} cost {o}",
        "floorArea": "{s} has a floor area of {o} square metres",
        "architect": "{s} was designed by {o}",
        "owner": "{s} is owned by {o}",
        "formerName": "{s} was formerly known as {o}",
        "height": "{s} reaches a height of {o} metres",
        "buildingType": "{s} is a {o}",
        "developer": "{s} was developed by {o}",
        "tenant": "{s} is tenanted by {o}",
        "isPartOf": "{s} is part of {o}",
        "country": "{s} is in {o}",
        "currentTenants": "{s} currently houses {o}",
        "address": "{s} is situated at {o}",
        "inaugurationDate": "{s} was inaugurated on {o}",
        "birthPlace": "{s} was born in {o}",
        "deathPlace": "{s} died in {o}",
        "bedCount": "{s} has {o} beds",
        "region": "{s} is in the {o} region",
        "state": "{s} is located in {o}",
        "website": "{s}'s website is {o}",
        "yearOfConstruction": "{s} was built in {o}",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": (
            "{s} has the NRHP reference number {o}"
        ),
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": (
            "{s} was added to the National Register of Historic Places on {o}"
        ),
        "significantBuilding": "{s} is known for the significant building {o}",
        "governingBody": "{s} is governed by {o}",
        "leader": "{s} is led by {o}",
        "chancellor": "{s} has chancellor {o}",
        "governmentType": "{s} has a {o} government",
        "capital": "{s}'s capital is {o}",
        "language": "{s} speaks {o}",
        "leaderTitle": "{s}'s leader holds the title {o}",
        "currency": "{s} uses the {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": "{s} includes the ethnic group {o}",
        "origin": "{s} originates from {o}",
        "significantProject": "{s} is known for the project {o}",
        "foundationPlace": "{s} was founded in {o}",
        "demonym": "{s} people are called {o}",
        "numberOfRooms": "{s} contains {o} rooms",
        "keyPerson": "{s} has key person {o}",
        "architecture": "{s} showcases {o} architecture",
        "postalCode": "{s} has postal code {o}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject to create concise clauses
    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for triple in triples:
        subject_groups[triple.subject].append(triple)

    clauses = []
    for subject, group in subject_groups.items():
        parts = []
        for triple in group:
            tmpl = templates.get(triple.predicate)
            if tmpl:
                phrase = tmpl.format(s=triple.subject, o=triple.object)
            else:
                phrase = f"{triple.subject} {triple.predicate} {triple.object}"
            parts.append(phrase.rstrip('.'))
        if len(parts) == 1:
            clause = parts[0]
        else:
            clause = ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1]
        clauses.append(clause)

    if not clauses:
        return ""
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        result = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        result = "; ".join(clauses[:-1]) + "; and " + clauses[-1] + "."
    return result[0].upper() + result[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

          sentences.append(sentence.rstrip('.'))
  
      # Combine sentences into one fluent paragraph
      if not sentences:

      # Join with commas and a final conjunction for readability
      combined = ", ".join(sentences[:-1]) + ", and " + sentences[-1] + "."
      # Capitalize first character
      return combined[0].upper() + combined[1:]
